title: Recent historical record of organic carbon burial rates and potentially toxic elements fluxes in an intermittently closed tropical lagoon (National Park in southern Mexican Pacific)
author: Carnero-Bravo, Vladislav
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This dataset comprises the geochemical characterization of a 210 Pb-dated sediment core (2PASTO22; 27 cm in length), including grain-size distribution, organic matter (OM) and carbonate contents, metal concentrations, the potentially toxic element enrichments and fluxes, along with organic carbon stocks (C org ) and burial rates. The core was collected on June 28, 2022, from Pastoría Coastal Lagoon (15°58′33″ N, 97°35′01″ W), located within the Chacahua Lagoons National Park (CLNP) in the southern Mexican Pacific.
steps_raw: This dataset comprises the geochemical characterization of a 210 Pb-dated sediment core (2PASTO22; 27 cm in length), including grain-size distribution, organic matter (OM) and carbonate contents, metal concentrations, the potentially toxic element enrichments and fluxes, along with organic carbon stocks (C org ) and burial rates. The core was collected on June 28, 2022, from Pastoría Coastal Lagoon (15°58′33″ N, 97°35′01″ W), located within the Chacahua Lagoons National Park (CLNP) in the southern Mexican Pacific.
